what_it_does: Renders a fixed 1600 by 900 visual composition titled "The Tools, Gathered and Handed On" with labeled movement verbs, an SVG circuit of tools, transport, gathered seams, a Press node, and an outward fan network generated through DNA.org helpers.
observations: Loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts.
Uses shared CSS variables from /surface/styles/vars.css for colors, type sizes, and spacing.
Builds a centered 1600x900 .view with a radial background, faint blueprint grid, title block, world field, footer wash, and circular V mark.
The SVG depicts a tool strip descending, a dashed transport line into a plug, three labeled seam circles converging into a Press polygon, and a fan network going outward to six labeled nodes.
Calls DNA.org.mesh, DNA.org.iconStrip, and DNA.org.hubNetwork from /surface/runtime/organisms.js to populate generated visual parts.
standouts: Movement is encoded through dashed lines, arrow markers, and verb captions: descend, transport, gather in, and gather out.
The Press polygon is treated as the visual hub for the outward fan while the generated hubNetwork is configured with hub:"none".
The scene combines hand-authored SVG structure with runtime-generated organism fragments.
conventions: Uses a self-contained HTML file with inline CSS and inline script.
Uses absolute positioning for a canvas-like presentation piece.
Uses ids as insertion points for runtime-generated SVG/HTML fragments.
Uses uppercase monospaced micro-labels for verb captions and sans-serif labels for node text.
concerns: The layout is fixed to 1600x900 and depends on hard-coded SVG and absolute coordinates.
The script assumes DNA.org and its organism helpers are present before execution.
The fan group has an initial transform in markup that is immediately overwritten in script, leaving the first value unused.
notes: A static visual ledger piece that diagrams movement verbs as a tool-to-press-to-hands flow.
questions: What semantic meanings do the fan node labels C, D, K, B, S, and F represent?
Is the overwritten fan transform intentional or leftover positioning work?
Should the Press be understood as an engine, hub, or named process in the ledger vocabulary?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the rendered content: the file visualizes movement verbs around tools being gathered, moved, pressed, and handed outward.
apparent_themes: generated organism graphics inside authored composition
intention_signals: It seems intended to make abstract movement verbs legible as one integrated process diagram, with the Press acting as the central action that receives seams and distributes kept work outward.
novelty: The file frames verbs as spatial operations in a single illustrated circuit, mixing socket/plug transport, seam convergence, and an outward hub network in one scene.
contribution: Contributes a movement-verb visualization substrate: a fixed presentation frame, authored SVG process geometry, generated mesh/tool/fan organisms, and labels that map descend, transport, gather-in, and gather-out into one ledger diagram.
summary_for_embedding: A fixed HTML/SVG presentation piece showing tools descending, transporting into a Press, seams gathering inward, and work fanning outward through generated DNA.org visual organisms.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for expressing movement verbs as a neutral diagrammatic flow of tools, seams, a central Press, and outward handoff nodes.
